courseTitle: Site Plan Drawing — Municipal Development Standards
courseDescription: Comprehensive guide to creating professional site plans for municipal submission. Covers property boundaries, building footprints, parking, landscaping, utilities, drainage, GPS integration, and all municipal requirements for building permits and site development approval.

practicalExamples.title: Residential Complex Site Planning
practicalExamples.description: Multi-family residential project with parking and landscaping
steps: Start with GPS coordinates for property corners
Use drone survey for accurate topography
Design parking layout per municipal code (1 space per 100 sq ft)
Include landscape areas meeting 30% green space requirement
Show utility connections with proper clearances

practicalExamples.title: Commercial Building Site Development
practicalExamples.description: Shopping complex with customer parking and service access
steps: Survey site using total station for accuracy
Design customer parking (5 spaces per 100 sq ft)
Include delivery truck access and loading zones
Show pedestrian circulation and ADA compliance
Design stormwater management per municipal standards

practicalExamples.title: Industrial Facility Site Layout
practicalExamples.description: Manufacturing plant with heavy vehicle access
steps: Use GPS/GIS for large site mapping
Design truck circulation and parking areas
Show utility corridors and service areas
Design worker parking and pedestrian safety

courseObjectives: Create complete site plans showing building placement and site development
Include parking layouts, landscaping, and utility connections
Show drainage patterns and stormwater management features
Produce municipal-compliant site plans with all required elements

phases.phaseTitle: Setup — Site Plan Standards and Layers
phases.phaseDescription: Prepare drawing environment with proper units, layers, and municipal standards.

steps.title: Configure Drawing Units and Scale
steps.subtitle: Set up for site plan requirements
steps.why: Site plans typically use 1:200 or 1:500 scale and require precise unit setup for accurate dimensions.
instructions: Type UNITS and set Type to Decimal, Insertion Scale to Meters
Set Precision to 0.00 for centimeter accuracy
Set LIMITS to accommodate entire site (e.g., 0,0 to 200,200 for 200m x 200m site)
Set annotation scale to 1:200 or 1:500 as required
Save as Site_Plan_Template.dwt for future projects

steps.title: Create Site Plan Layer System
steps.subtitle: Organize all site elements
steps.why: Proper layer organization is essential for controlling visibility, printing, and municipal review.
instructions: Create property layers: PROPERTY_LINE (Red, 0.50mm), EASEMENT (Yellow, 0.30mm)
Create building layers: BUILDING_FOOTPRINT (White, 0.40mm), BUILDING_OUTLINE (Cyan, 0.25mm)
Create parking layers: PARKING_STALLS (Blue, 0.25mm), PARKING_MARKING (White, 0.18mm)
Create landscape layers: LANDSCAPING (Green, 0.20mm), TREES (Dark Green, 0.25mm)
Create utility layers: WATER_LINE (Blue, 0.30mm), SEWER_LINE (Brown, 0.30mm), ELECTRIC (Red, 0.25mm)
Create drainage layers: DRAINAGE (Cyan, 0.35mm), CONTOURS (Gray, 0.13mm)
Create annotation layers: TEXT (White, 0.18mm), DIMENSIONS (Yellow, 0.18mm), HATCH (Dark Gray, 0.13mm)

phases.phaseDescription: Draw property lines and incorporate survey information.

steps.title: Draw Property Boundaries with GPS Integration
steps.subtitle: Define legal property limits using modern survey methods
steps.why: Property boundaries must be accurate as they define the legal limits of development and must match land registry records.
instructions: Set current layer to PROPERTY_LINE
Method 1: GPS Survey - Mark corners with GPS device, import coordinates using POINT command
Method 2: Total Station - Import survey data from professional surveyor
Method 3: Land Registry - Use coordinates from official land registration documents
Draw boundary using PLINE connecting coordinate points
Add bearing and distance annotations (e.g., 'N 45°30'15" E 25.50m')
Include GPS coordinates at each corner (e.g., '27.7172°N, 85.3240°E')
Calculate total area using AREA command and annotate

practicalTip.title: Coordinate System for Nepal
practicalTip.text: Use UTM Zone 45N with WGS84 datum for all municipal submissions in Nepal. Convert local coordinates to UTM using online tools or GIS software.

steps.title: Add Easements and Setbacks
steps.subtitle: Show legal restrictions and building limits
steps.why: Easements and setbacks are legal requirements that restrict where buildings can be placed.
instructions: Set current layer to EASEMENT
Use OFFSET to create setback lines from property boundaries (typically 3-6m)
Draw utility easements using different linetypes if required
Hatch setback areas with light pattern to show building restriction zones
Add setback dimension annotations
Label different easement types (utility, drainage, access)

steps.title: Import or Draw Topography
steps.subtitle: Show site elevation and contours
steps.why: Topography affects building placement, drainage, and site design decisions.
instructions: Set current layer to CONTOURS
Import survey contours if available, or draw manually using SPLINE
Set contour interval (typically 0.5m or 1m depending on site slope)
Add elevation labels at regular intervals along contours
Show high and low points clearly
Add spot elevations at key locations (building corners, access points)

phases.phaseDescription: Position buildings and show their relationship to the site.

steps.title: Draw Building Footprints
steps.subtitle: Show building outline and floor area
steps.why: Building footprints show the exact location and size of structures on the site.
instructions: Set current layer to BUILDING_FOOTPRINT
Draw building outline using RECTANG or PLINE based on architectural plans
Ensure building is within all setback requirements
Add building dimensions (length, width)
Calculate and annotate building footprint area
Show building entrance locations and main access points

steps.title: Add Building Details and Overhangs
steps.subtitle: Show architectural features affecting site
steps.why: Overhangs, balconies, and projections affect setback calculations and neighbor relationships.
instructions: Use BUILDING_OUTLINE layer for architectural projections
Draw roof overhangs, balconies, and bay windows using OFFSET
Show different building levels if multiple floors
Hatch different floor areas with distinct patterns if required
Add building height annotation and floor count

phases.phaseDescription: Design parking areas and vehicle circulation patterns.

steps.title: Layout Parking Areas per Municipal Code
steps.subtitle: Design parking stalls meeting accessibility and efficiency requirements
steps.why: Adequate parking is a municipal requirement and must comply with accessibility laws and efficiency standards.
instructions: Set current layer to PARKING_STALLS
Calculate required spaces: Residential (1:100 sq ft), Commercial (5:100 sq ft), Industrial (3:100 sq ft)
Draw standard spaces (2.4m x 4.8m) and compact spaces (2.3m x 4.2m)
Design accessible spaces (3.6m x 4.8m) - 5% of total spaces minimum
Use ARRAY for efficient layout of multiple rows
Add access aisles (6m for two-way, 4.5m for one-way)
Hatch accessible spaces with distinctive pattern
Add directional arrows and parking space numbers

steps.title: Add Parking Markings and Symbols
steps.subtitle: Complete parking layout details
steps.why: Clear markings and symbols help reviewers understand parking layout and traffic flow.
instructions: Set current layer to PARKING_MARKING
Draw stall lines using LINE with appropriate spacing
Add parking space numbers if required
Show directional arrows for traffic flow
Mark disabled spaces with appropriate symbols
Add parking area labels and capacity information

steps.title: Design Vehicle Circulation
steps.subtitle: Show access routes and driveways
steps.why: Proper vehicle circulation ensures safe access and meets municipal traffic requirements.
instructions: Draw driveway access from public road to site
Design internal circulation roads with appropriate radii
Show turning radii for vehicles (typically 6m minimum)
Add pedestrian walkways separate from vehicle traffic
Ensure emergency access routes are clearly shown

phases.phaseDescription: Add landscaping elements and site amenities.

steps.title: Add Landscaping Areas
steps.subtitle: Show green spaces and planting areas
steps.why: Landscaping is often required by municipalities and improves site aesthetics.
instructions: Set current layer to LANDSCAPING
Draw lawn areas, garden beds, and green spaces
Use HATCH with grass pattern for lawn areas
Show planting beds with different hatching patterns
Add landscape area calculations and percentages
Ensure landscaping meets municipal minimum requirements

steps.title: Place Trees and Planting
steps.subtitle: Show individual trees and major plantings
steps.why: Trees provide shade, improve aesthetics, and may be required by municipal landscaping standards.
instructions: Set current layer to TREES
Draw tree circles (canopy spread) using CIRCLE
Use different sizes for mature vs. new trees
Show existing trees to remain vs. new trees to be planted
Add tree species labels if required
Ensure tree placement doesn't conflict with utilities or structures

phases.phaseDescription: Show utility connections and service infrastructure.

steps.title: Draw Water and Sewer Connections
steps.subtitle: Show utility service lines
steps.why: Utility connections are essential for building occupancy and must be properly shown.
instructions: Set current layer to WATER_LINE
Draw water main connection from street to building
Show water meter location and shutoff valves
Switch to SEWER_LINE layer and draw sewer connection
Show cleanout locations and sewer line slope
Add pipe size annotations and connection points

steps.title: Add Electrical and Other Utilities
steps.subtitle: Complete utility infrastructure
steps.why: All utility services must be shown for complete site plan approval.
instructions: Set current layer to ELECTRIC
Draw electrical service connection from pole/transformer to building
Show meter location and service entrance
Add other utilities like gas, telephone, or cable if applicable
Show utility easements and clearance requirements
Label all utility lines clearly

phases.phaseDescription: Design drainage patterns and stormwater management features.

steps.title: Design Surface Drainage
steps.subtitle: Show water flow patterns
steps.why: Proper drainage prevents flooding and is a critical municipal requirement.
instructions: Set current layer to DRAINAGE
Draw surface drainage flow arrows using PLINE
Show direction of water flow across the site
Indicate low points and collection areas
Show how water flows to storm drains or swales
Ensure drainage doesn't cause problems for neighboring properties

steps.title: Add Stormwater Management Features
steps.subtitle: Show drainage infrastructure
steps.why: Stormwater management features are often required for site development approval.
instructions: Draw stormwater collection pits or catch basins
Show swales or drainage channels if required
Add detention ponds or rain gardens if part of design
Show infiltration areas or permeable surfaces
Add drainage calculations and capacity information

phases.phaseDescription: Complete the site plan with annotations, dimensions, and required elements.

steps.title: Add Dimensions and Annotations
steps.subtitle: Complete technical documentation
steps.why: Clear dimensions and annotations are essential for municipal review and construction.
instructions: Set current layer to DIMENSIONS
Add overall site dimensions
Dimension building setbacks from property lines
Add parking space dimensions and aisle widths
Annotate landscape areas and tree locations
Add utility line sizes and slopes
Include all required municipal annotations

steps.title: Add North Arrow and Scale
steps.subtitle: Essential map elements
steps.why: North arrow and scale are mandatory for all site plan submissions.
instructions: Insert professional north arrow pointing to true north
Add scale bar appropriate to drawing scale (1:200 or 1:500)
Include written scale notation (e.g., 'Scale 1:200')
Place both elements in non-obtrusive locations

steps.title: Create Title Block and Legend
steps.subtitle: Complete submission documentation
steps.why: Title block and legend provide essential project information and symbol explanations.
instructions: Create title block with project name, site plan title, scale, date
Include drawing number, prepared by, checked by information
Add legend explaining all symbols, hatching patterns, and line types
Include municipal reference numbers and application details
Add contact information for project team

steps.title: Final Municipal Compliance Check
steps.subtitle: Verify all requirements with GPS verification
steps.why: Municipalities reject incomplete or non-compliant site plans and require coordinate verification.
instructions: Verify all setback requirements are met with precise measurements
Check parking count meets municipal code requirements
Ensure landscaping percentage meets minimum standards (30% for residential)
Confirm all utilities are properly shown with clearances
Check that drainage design handles 100-year storm events
Verify GPS coordinates match land registry records
Prepare coordinate verification sheet with corner points
Create PDF submission with layers optimized for municipal review
submissionChecklist: GPS coordinates at all property corners
